title: phebalium squamulosum var. alpinum
intro: Phebalium squamulosum var. alpinum, commonly known as the alpine Phebalium or a variety of the Phebalium squamulosum plant, is a flowering plant species in the family Rutaceae, native to southeastern Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Phebalium squamulosum var. alpinum include its potential to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as its use as a treatment for skin conditions and wounds. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
